prevalence.trend: stable; chronic urticaria recognized more often

incidence.source: Primary care and allergy clinic data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant itch and sleep disturbance

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical wheals lasting <24h; chronic urticaria defined as >6 weeks

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions with acute episodes; hundreds of thousands with chronic disease
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improved with guideline-based antihistamine escalation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; limited lab work-up for chronic cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Second-generation antihistamines, dose escalation; omalizumab for refractory chronic urticaria where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to allergy specialists and biologics
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Antihistamines widely available and effective for most cases
